prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards present in this scenario before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 081755Z 31022G38KT 2SM -RA SCT008 BKN015 OVC040 07/05 A2982
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 081720Z 0818/0918 31018G30KT 3SM -RA BKN015
  TEMPO 0820/0824 25G40KT 1SM -RA OVC006
Runway in use: 28L (heading 280°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121 dispatch, ETA 0821Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) Current wind 310/22G38 — crosswind on 28L (angle 310-280=30°): gust crosswind = 38 × sin(30°) = 19 kt from right. Below 25 kt limit but close. (2) TEMPO 0820/0824 wind 250G40 — angle on 28L = 250-280 = -30° (30° left); gust crosswind = 40 × sin(30°) = 20 kt from left. Still below 25 kt but in TEMPO during ETA. (3) TEMPO during ETA (0821Z falls within TEMPO 0820-0824Z): vis drops to 1SM and ceiling OVC006 (600 ft) — below CAT I minima (vis must be ≥ ½ SM=805m and ceiling ≥ 200 ft; 600 ft ceiling is above 200 ft DH but vis 1SM is below 3SM for alternate triggering). (4) Current vis 2SM and ceiling BKN015 = IFR — alternate required since destination is below 2000/3 during ETA window. (5) Light rain (-RA) — runway may be wet, braking degraded. (6) Temp 7°C / dewpoint 5°C — spread 2°C, fog or mist risk.
required_safety_constraints: All wind hazards must use gust speed, not mean wind (FAA-H-8083-25C).
TEMPO during ETA window must be assessed — 1SM vis and OVC006 during 0820-0824Z triggers alternate requirement (14 CFR §121.619).
Ceiling BKN015 at destination during ETA is below 2000 ft — alternate required.
Crosswind must be evaluated for both current wind and TEMPO gust conditions.
Rain-wet runway affects braking — noted but outside weather minima tool scope.
acceptable_variants: Hazards include: crosswind near limit in TEMPO, TEMPO IFR conditions during ETA, alternate required, wet runway, fog risk.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: METAR and TAF constructed with TEMPO onset coinciding with ETA, gusts near crosswind limit, IFR conditions. Designed to test: gust crosswind, TEMPO temporal coincidence with ETA, alternate requirement.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 140630Z 09005KT 10SM CLR 22/18 A3005
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 140540Z 1406/1506 08006KT 9999 SKC
  FM141300 22010KT 9999 FEW040
  TEMPO 1417/1421 VRB15G28KT 2SM TSRA BKN025CB
Runway in use: 09R (heading 090°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1419Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) TEMPO 1417-1421Z includes ETA 1419Z: conditions during ETA are VRB15G28KT with TSRA (thunderstorm with rain) and BKN025CB. (2) Thunderstorm (TS) at destination during ETA — CRITICAL. Thunderstorms require immediate evaluation; TS at destination during ETA may require hold, divert, or delay. SIGMETs must be checked. (3) BKN025CB: cumulonimbus in ceiling — thunderstorm confirmed. CB in approach path is a severe hazard (turbulence, wind shear, lightning). (4) VRB15G28KT — variable direction gust 28 kt on runway 090°: worst-case crosswind on 09R = 28 × sin(90°) = 28 kt from any direction — EXCEEDS 20 kt crosswind limit. (5) Vis 2SM during TEMPO — below 3SM, triggering alternate requirement under §121.619. (6) Temp 22°C / dewpoint 18°C — spread only 4°C; high moisture, convection risk consistent with thunderstorm forecast.
required_safety_constraints: TS/CB at destination during ETA is a critical hazard; SIGMET check mandatory (FAA-AC-00-45H).
VRB15G28KT: worst-case crosswind = 28 kt on any runway — EXCEEDS 20 kt aircraft limit (FAA-H-8083-25C).
Alternate required: vis 2SM < 3SM during ETA window (14 CFR §121.619).
CB layer constitutes a ceiling hazard and severe weather threat.
All TEMPO conditions during ETA must be treated as operationally credible.
acceptable_variants: Hazards: TS/CB at ETA (critical), VRB gusts exceeding crosswind limit, TEMPO vis below alternate threshold, alternate required, SIGMET check needed.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: thunderstorm TEMPO timed to coincide with ETA, VRB gusts exceeding crosswind limit, multiple simultaneous hazards including CB. Tests TS recognition and VRB worst-case crosswind.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 220400Z 00000KT 1/4SM FG OVC002 02/02 A3010
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 220320Z 2204/2304 00000KT 0800 FG OVC002
  BECMG 2209/2211 VRB03KT 3SM BR BKN010
Runway in use: 10R (heading 100°)
Aircraft CAT I authorized only
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 0500Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) Current METAR: vis 1/4SM (≈402m) and OVC002 (200 ft ceiling). These are BELOW CAT I minima: vis 1/4SM < ½SM (CAT I requires ≥½SM or 1800 ft RVR); ceiling 200ft = exactly CAT I DH (marginal). The 1/4SM visibility is the critical limiter — current conditions are below CAT I approach minima for visibility. (2) TAF base: fog (FG) 0800m vis, OVC002 throughout 2204-2304Z. Forecast at ETA 0500Z = still fog OVC002 with 0800m vis. 0800m ≈ 2624ft — above 1800 ft CAT I RVR minimum. Ceiling OVC002 = 200 ft = CAT I DH (marginal/GO-but-monitor). (3) BECMG improvement not until 0900-1100Z — ETA 0500Z is before improvement. Conditions forecast to remain below or at CAT I minima at ETA. (4) Temperature 2°C = dewpoint 2°C — temperature-dewpoint spread zero: dense fog risk, conditions unlikely to improve before BECMG. (5) No wind (00000KT) — no natural fog dispersal mechanism. (6) Alternate required: destination forecast below 2000 ft ceiling and below 3 SM throughout ETA window (§121.619).
required_safety_constraints: Current vis 1/4SM is below CAT I minimum of ½SM — NO-GO currently under CAT I rules (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Forecast at ETA maintains fog; operations require CAT I or better conditions at minimums.
Alternate required: forecast below 2000 ft / 3SM at ETA under §121.619.
Zero temp-dewpoint spread = dense persistent fog — note for trend assessment.
BECMG improvement is AFTER ETA; do not apply BECMG conditions to ETA period.
acceptable_variants: Current vis below CAT I; ETA forecast at/near CAT I minimum; alternate required; fog persistent until after ETA; delay or divert recommended.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: dense fog scenario with BECMG improvement after ETA. Tests: CAT I minima check, BECMG timing vs ETA, no-wind-no-dispersal assessment.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 051200Z 25012G22KT 8SM -SN OVC018 M03/M07 A2990
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 051140Z 0512/0612 25014G25KT 5SM -SN OVC015
  FM051600 27010KT 3SM SN OVC010
  FM051900 28005KT 1SM -SN FZFG OVC004
Runway in use: 28L (heading 280°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1700Z
gold_decision: Hazards at current time and ETA: (1) ETA 1700Z falls just after FM051600 group onset: wind 270/10, vis 3SM (IFR), SN (snow), OVC010 (1000 ft ceiling). Vis 3SM is exactly at the §121.619 threshold — forecast at ETA at the boundary triggers alternate requirement (below 3SM). (2) FM051900 (after ETA): deterioration to vis 1SM + FZFG (freezing fog) + OVC004 (400 ft ceiling) — although after ETA, this reflects forecast trend towards LIFR conditions and icing. (3) Snow (SN) at ETA: runway contamination, braking action degraded. Aircraft icing risk with -3°C temps. (4) FZFG (freezing fog) in late forecast — extremely hazardous icing; indicates potential for rapidly worsening icing conditions during approach and taxi. (5) Current crosswind check: wind 250/12G22 on runway 280°: angle = 250-280 = -30°; gust crosswind = 22 × sin(30°) = 11 kt from left — within 25 kt limit. At ETA: 270/10 on 280°: angle = -10°; crosswind = 10 × sin(10°) ≈ 1.7 kt — negligible. (6) Temp -3°C / dewpoint -7°C — icing conditions throughout.
required_safety_constraints: FM 1700 group shows vis 3SM (at §121.619 boundary) — alternate required.
FM 1900 forecasts FZFG + SN + OVC004 = LIFR conditions with severe icing.
SN and sub-zero temps require de-icing/anti-icing pre-departure and holdover time planning.
FZFG is a critical hazard even if after ETA — indicates rapidly deteriorating icing environment.
Crosswind within limits at both current and ETA conditions.
acceptable_variants: Alternate required (vis at boundary at ETA); FZFG in trend; icing conditions; runway contamination; crosswind within limits.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: progressive deterioration scenario with FZFG onset after ETA. Tests: FM timing boundary with §121.619, icing trend recognition, gust crosswind within limit.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 111830Z 18008KT 10SM BKN050 25/14 A2998
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 111800Z 1118/1218 18010KT 9999 BKN050
  PROB40 1120/1122 TSRA OVC020CB
  FM112200 20015G25KT 9999 SCT040
Runway in use: 18L (heading 180°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 2045Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) ETA 2045Z is within PROB40 1120/1122 (2000-2200Z) window: 40% probability of TSRA (thunderstorm with rain) and OVC020CB at ETA. CB (cumulonimbus) = thunderstorm — safety-critical. Must check SIGMETs. (2) PROB40 is treated as operationally credible (40% probability of thunderstorm); alternate planning must account for possible TS at ETA. (3) FM112200 comes just after ETA — wind 200/15G25 on runway 180°: angle = 200-180=20°; gust crosswind = 25 × sin(20°) ≈ 8.6 kt — within 20 kt limit. But this FM group begins at 2200Z, after ETA of 2045Z, so base forecast (18010KT) applies at ETA. (4) At ETA under base forecast: wind 180/10 on runway 180°: direct headwind, zero crosswind — benign wind. (5) Temperature 25°C / dewpoint 14°C — spread 11°C; convective development possible (consistent with PROB40 TS). (6) If PROB40 TS occurs: vis/ceiling not stated in PROB40 group but OVC020CB implies ceiling 2000 ft — above CAT I minimum. Thunderstorm itself is primary hazard. (7) Alternate: base forecast has BKN050 and 9999 throughout — conditions are above 2000/3SM, so alternate not required under base forecast. However, PROB40 TS could change this operationally.
required_safety_constraints: PROB40 TS/CB during ETA window must be treated as credible risk; SIGMET/PIREP review required (FAA-AC-00-45H).
CB in approach path is severe weather hazard regardless of ceiling or visibility values.
Wind at ETA is from base forecast (FM group comes after ETA); base wind is direct headwind, benign.
Convective conditions require human escalation for dispatch authorization.
acceptable_variants: Primary hazard: PROB40 TS/CB during ETA. No alternate required under base forecast. Wind benign. Escalation needed for TS risk.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: PROB40 TS/CB timed to overlap ETA window; FM group just after ETA. Tests PROB40 credibility assessment and FM timing boundary.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 030900Z 07035G55KT 15SM CLR 18/03 A3012
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 030840Z 0309/0409 07030G50KT 9999 SKC
  FM031400 06020G30KT 9999 FEW050
Runway in use: 09R (heading 090°) — only runway available
Aircraft max crosswind limit: 25 kt
Aircraft max tailwind limit: 10 kt
Flight rule: VFR, Part 91, ETA 1030Z
Note: Aircraft is departing KZZZ (not landing)
gold_decision: Hazards at departure: (1) Wind 070/35G55 on departure runway 09R (heading 090°): angle = 070-090 = -20°. Gust crosswind = 55 × sin(20°) ≈ 55 × 0.342 ≈ 18.8 kt from left. Within 25 kt limit — but marginal. Gust headwind = 55 × cos(20°) ≈ 55 × 0.940 ≈ 51.7 kt — extreme headwind gust. (2) Mean wind crosswind = 35 × sin(20°) ≈ 12 kt; mean headwind = 35 × cos(20°) ≈ 32.9 kt. (3) Wind 35G55 is exceptionally strong — structural loads, taxiing hazard, control during takeoff roll. Even if crosswind within certification limit, 55 kt gusts are operationally hazardous and require Captain/PIC evaluation. (4) Low humidity (temp 18, dew 3, spread 15°C) — no precipitation risk, but gusty dry conditions. (5) Only one runway available — no alternative runway alignment to reduce crosswind. (6) Forecast at departure (0900Z falls in base forecast): 30G50 — even mean gust 50 kt is extreme. (7) Pilot escalation / operational risk assessment required — gust 55 kt even with crosswind within limit is outside normal operations for many aircraft.
required_safety_constraints: Use gust speed (55 kt) for crosswind calculation: ~19 kt from left (FAA-H-8083-25C).
55 kt total wind speed with gusts is extreme — structural and control risk during ground roll.
No alternate runway available — no way to align more favorably.
Escalation to Captain/PIC and operational management is required for 55 kt gust departure.
acceptable_variants: Crosswind within limit but gust 55 kt is extreme; structural risk; escalation required. No alternate runway.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: extreme wind scenario (35G55) with only one runway. Tests: gust crosswind calculation, recognition that extreme gusts require escalation even if crosswind is within published limit.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 201400Z 28010KT 7SM OVC045 12/08 A2995
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 201340Z 2014/2114 28008KT 9999 BKN045
  BECMG 2018/2020 25005KT 9999 SKC
  TEMPO 2101/2105 VRB20G32KT 1SM -FZRA FG OVC003
Runway in use: 28L (heading 280°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt, CAT I authorized
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 2103Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) ETA 2103Z falls within TEMPO 2101/2105 (0100-0500Z on day 21): conditions are VRB20G32KT, vis 1SM, -FZRA (freezing rain), FG (fog), OVC003 (300 ft ceiling). (2) FZRA (freezing rain) during ETA — critical icing hazard. Aircraft may be exposed to FZRA during approach; de-icing holdover times are shortened or ineffective in FZRA. (3) OVC003 = 300 ft ceiling (above CAT I DH of 200 ft — marginal). Vis 1SM with FZRA and FG during TEMPO. (4) VRB20G32KT: worst-case crosswind on 28L = 32 kt (if wind 90° off 280° heading) — could EXCEED 25 kt limit. Variable direction means worst-case must be assumed. (5) FG + FZRA combination: runway surface may be ice-contaminated (braking action). (6) BECMG improvement between 1800-2000Z to clear — this precedes ETA at 2103Z, but TEMPO then reasserts adverse conditions. (7) Alternate required: TEMPO at ETA shows vis 1SM < 3SM and ceiling 300 ft < 2000 ft (§121.619).
required_safety_constraints: FZRA during approach = critical icing; escalation and divert planning required.
VRB32 gust worst-case crosswind may EXCEED 25 kt certified limit (FAA-H-8083-25C).
Alternate required: TEMPO at ETA forecasts below §121.619 threshold (14 CFR §121.619).
BECMG improvement is between 1800-2000Z; this does not prevent TEMPO adverse conditions at ETA 2103Z.
OVC003 ceiling is above CAT I DH but combined with vis 1SM and FZRA is operationally severe.
acceptable_variants: Critical hazards: FZRA during TEMPO at ETA, VRB gusts potentially exceeding crosswind limit, alternate required, icing hazard.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: FZRA TEMPO at ETA; BECMG improvement before ETA then TEMPO degrades again. Tests: BECMG vs TEMPO interaction, VRB crosswind worst-case, FZRA severity.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 092100Z 19006KT 10SM BKN070 20/12 A3001
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 092040Z 0921/1021 19006KT 9999 BKN060
  FM100200 22012KT 6SM -RA BKN030
  FM100600 23018G28KT 2SM -RA BKN012 OVC025
  FM101000 24022G35KT 1SM -RA FG OVC006
Runway in use: 19L (heading 190°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt, CAT I authorized
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1000Z (next day)
gold_decision: Hazards at ETA (1000Z = FM101000 group): (1) Wind 240/22G35 on runway 190°: angle = 240-190=50°; gust crosswind = 35 × sin(50°) ≈ 35 × 0.766 ≈ 26.8 kt — EXCEEDS 20 kt crosswind limit. (2) Visibility 1SM with -RA (light rain) and FG (fog) = LIFR conditions. Below CAT I vis minimum of ½SM — actually 1SM > ½SM, so above CAT I vis minimum. But OVC006 = 600 ft ceiling (well above CAT I DH 200 ft). (3) Vis 1SM is below 3SM — alternate required under §121.619. (4) Progressive deterioration through three FM groups — each worse than prior. ETA conditions are the worst in the forecast. (5) By 1000Z: light rain + fog with OVC006 — potential ILS approaches marginal. (6) Trend: conditions steadily worsen from 2100Z current through ETA — no improvement forecast before or at ETA. Destination approach at ETA will be IFR with crosswind exceeding aircraft limit.
required_safety_constraints: ETA crosswind exceeds 20 kt limit: gust crosswind ≈ 26.8 kt (FAA-H-8083-25C).
Alternate required: vis 1SM at ETA is below 3SM threshold (14 CFR §121.619).
Progressive deterioration: all three FM groups degrade further — trend awareness required.
Ceiling 600 ft at ETA is above CAT I DH (200 ft) but crosswind is the limiting factor.
acceptable_variants: Crosswind at ETA exceeds limit; alternate required; progressive deterioration; IFR/LIFR at ETA.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: three FM groups of progressive deterioration; ETA falls on worst group with crosswind exceeding limit. Tests trend recognition and correct ETA-group selection.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 171530Z 35015G24KT 4SM BR OVC012 08/07 A2988
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 171510Z 1715/1815 35012KT 5SM BR OVC015
  TEMPO 1718/1722 00000KT 1/4SM FG OVC002
  BECMG 1723/1801 36008KT 6SM BR BKN030
Runway in use: 36 (heading 360°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt, CAT I authorized
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1750Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) ETA 1750Z is NOT in TEMPO 1718/1722 (ends 2200Z). Wait — TEMPO 1718/1722 = 1800Z to 2200Z? No: 1718 = day 17 at 1800Z? Actually, in TAF notation DDhh, TEMPO 1718 = day 17 at 1800Z, TEMPO end 1722 = day 17 at 2200Z. ETA 1750Z is on day 17 at 1750Z, which IS within TEMPO 1718/1722 (1800Z–2200Z). Wait, 1750Z < 1800Z so ETA 1750Z is BEFORE the TEMPO window. Therefore ETA falls in base forecast, not TEMPO. (2) Under base forecast at 1750Z: 35012KT, 5SM, BR, OVC015. Wind 350/12 on runway 360°: angle = 350-360 = -10°; crosswind = 12 × sin(10°) ≈ 2.1 kt — negligible. Headwind = 12 × cos(10°) ≈ 11.8 kt. (3) Vis 5SM = VFR; ceiling OVC015 = 1500 ft. Conditions at ETA are marginal VFR/IFR (BKN/OVC below 3000 ft = IFR). (4) TEMPO starts at 1800Z — 10 minutes after ETA. Conditions in TEMPO are: 1/4SM FG OVC002 (200 ft ceiling) — LIFR/CAT I boundary. These conditions are just after ETA; runway conditions during taxi-in/gate arrival may be affected. (5) Temp 8°C / dewpoint 7°C: spread 1°C — persistent fog/mist risk entirely consistent with TEMPO fog forecast. (6) Current METAR gust 24 kt on runway 360°: crosswind = 24 × sin(10°) ≈ 4.2 kt — negligible. (7) Alternate requirement check: TEMPO just outside ETA window — technically ETA is in base forecast (5SM, OVC015). But the TEMPO onset is very close to ETA and should be flagged as a risk.
required_safety_constraints: ETA 1750Z is before TEMPO 1800Z onset; base conditions apply at ETA — correct temporal reasoning required.
TEMPO fog (1/4SM, OVC002) starts 10 minutes after ETA — very close; landing may be affected by onset of TEMPO.
Base forecast has OVC015 — IFR ceiling; alternate check: vis 5SM and ceiling 1500 ft are above 2000/3SM threshold — no alternate strictly required under §121.619 base forecast.
Low temp-dewpoint spread (1°C) confirms persistent fog risk consistent with TEMPO.
Flag TEMPO onset proximity to ETA as operational risk even though not in TEMPO at ETA.
acceptable_variants: ETA is in base forecast (before TEMPO); conditions marginal IFR but above §121.619 alternate trigger. TEMPO onset just after ETA is a risk. Low spread = persistent fog.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: TEMPO onset set 10 minutes after ETA to create a near-miss temporal hazard. Tests: correct ETA-to-TAF group mapping, proximity risk flagging.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 260500Z 09018G28KT 10SM SKC 15/02 A3002
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 260440Z 2605/2705 09016G26KT 9999 SKC
  FM261200 13020G32KT 9999 FEW030
  FM261800 16025G40KT 9999 SCT030
Runway in use: Only runway available is 10L (heading 100°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1830Z
gold_decision: Hazards at ETA (FM261800 group: wind 160/25G40, 9999, SCT030): (1) ETA 1830Z falls in FM261800 (valid from 1800Z onwards): wind 160/25G40 on runway 100°. Angle = 160-100=60°; gust crosswind = 40 × sin(60°) ≈ 40 × 0.866 ≈ 34.6 kt — EXCEEDS 25 kt crosswind limit significantly. This is a hard constraint: operation is NO-GO under crosswind limit with this wind and runway. (2) No alternate runway available — wind cannot be resolved by selecting different runway. (3) Mean crosswind = 25 × sin(60°) ≈ 21.7 kt — within limit for mean wind, but gust crosswind 34.6 kt is the operative limit. (4) Conditions are otherwise VFR (9999 vis, SCT030) — weather itself is not limiting, but crosswind is. (5) Note: FM261200 wind 130/20G32 on runway 100°: angle=30°; gust XW = 32 × sin(30°) = 16 kt — within limit at 1200Z. Conditions worsen towards ETA.
required_safety_constraints: Gust crosswind at ETA ≈ 34.6 kt EXCEEDS 25 kt limit — NO-GO for crosswind (FAA-H-8083-25C).
No alternate runway — cannot resolve crosswind by realigning.
Use gust speed not mean wind for limit check.
Recommend delay or divert if crosswind exceeds certified limit.
acceptable_variants: Crosswind at ETA exceeds limit (34.6 kt vs 25 kt max); no alternate runway; operation not authorized under crosswind limit.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: FM group at ETA with wind direction 60° off single available runway at gust speed that clearly exceeds 25 kt limit. Tests: gust crosswind calculation with non-trivial angle (sin 60°), recognition of crosswind limit exceedance.

prompt:

gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 0330Z (FM140300 group: wind 260/18G30, vis 2SM, -RA, BKN012/OVC020): (1) Wind 260/18G30 on runway 280°: angle=260-280=-20°; gust crosswind=30×sin(20°)≈10.3 kt from left — within 20 kt limit. Mean crosswind=18×sin(20°)≈6.2 kt. (2) Vis 2SM at ETA: below 3SM — alternate required under §121.619. (3) Ceiling BKN012 = 1200 ft — well above CAT I DH 200 ft; ceiling not limiting under CAT I. Ceiling not limiting under CAT I at ETA.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: ETA in FM140300 group; subsequent FM140800 shows further deterioration. Tests: correct ETA group identification in a three-FM-group TAF.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 071800Z 00000KT 1/4SM FZFG OVC001 M02/M02 A3020
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 071740Z 0718/0818 00000KT 0400 FZFG OVC001
  BECMG 0808/0810 VRB03KT 1500 BR OVC008
Runway in use: 09R (heading 090°)
Aircraft CAT I authorized only
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 0730Z
gold_decision: Hazards: (1) Current conditions: vis 1/4SM (402m ≈ 1319 ft < CAT I 1800 ft RVR), FZFG (freezing fog), OVC001 (100 ft ceiling — BELOW CAT I DH of 200 ft). Current conditions are below CAT I minima on BOTH ceiling (100 ft < 200 ft) and visibility (1319 ft < 1800 ft). Current operations at this airport are not authorized under CAT I rules. (2) TAF base: same conditions (0400m FZFG OVC001) until BECMG 0808/0810. ETA 0730Z is before BECMG improvement (starts 0800Z). Conditions at ETA = same LIFR FZFG OVC001 — still below CAT I minima. (3) FZFG is an extreme icing hazard: aircraft exposed to FZFG during approach will accumulate ice rapidly. De-icing effectiveness is nil in active FZFG. (4) Temp -2°C = dewpoint -2°C: zero spread — persistent saturated freezing conditions. (5) BECMG improvement not until after ETA; conditions not improving at ETA. (6) No wind (00000KT) — no natural dispersal mechanism. (7) This flight should not depart for this destination under these forecast conditions with only CAT I authorization.
required_safety_constraints: Current and forecast ceiling (100 ft) is BELOW CAT I DH (200 ft) — NO-GO for CAT I (FAA AIM §5-4-7).
Current and forecast visibility (1/4SM ≈ 1319 ft) is BELOW CAT I RVR minimum (1800 ft) — NO-GO.
FZFG is a severe icing hazard that creates unsafe approach conditions.
BECMG improvement is after ETA — cannot be assumed for ETA planning.
This scenario requires dispatch hold or divert decision.
acceptable_variants: Below CAT I minima on ceiling and vis at ETA; FZFG extreme icing hazard; BECMG after ETA; dispatch hold or divert required.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §5-4-7: CAT I minima (DH 200 ft, RVR 1800 ft).

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: both ceiling and vis below CAT I minima; FZFG; BECMG improvement after ETA. Tests: dual minima failure (both ceiling AND vis below CAT I), FZFG severity recognition.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 181000Z 15012KT 10SM FEW250 32/15 A3008
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 180940Z 1810/1910 15010KT 9999 FEW250
  PROB40 1814/1818 TSRA BKN020CB
  FM181800 16010KT 9999 SCT250
Runway in use: 15L (heading 150°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1615Z
gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 1615Z (within PROB40 1814/1818 window of 1400-1800Z): (1) PROB40 1814/1818 includes ETA 1615Z: 40% probability of TSRA (thunderstorm/rain) with BKN020CB. Thunderstorm with CB during approach — critical hazard. SIGMET check required. (2) FM181800 improvement to SCT250 starts at 1800Z — after the end of PROB40 window. ETA is well within PROB40 window. (3) Under base forecast at ETA: 15010KT, 9999, FEW250 — wind 150/10 on runway 150°: direct headwind, zero crosswind. (4) Temp 32°C / dewpoint 15°C: spread 17°C, potential for convective development consistent with PROB40 TS. High temperature = high density altitude effect. (5) Under PROB40 conditions: BKN020CB = ceiling 2000 ft (at the §121.619 threshold). CB = cumulonimbus, thunderstorm present. Vis not stated in PROB40 group — implied degradation from TSRA; exact vis unknown. (6) Alternate under base forecast: 9999 and FEW250 = no alternate required. Under PROB40 with uncertainty: operational judgment required.
required_safety_constraints: PROB40 TS/CB during ETA window must be treated as credible risk; SIGMET/PIREP review required.
CB at approach = severe weather hazard regardless of base forecast conditions.
FM1800 improvement is AFTER ETA — cannot be applied.
High temperature (32°C) and convective risk from temp-dewpoint spread — consistent with PROB40 TS.
acceptable_variants: PROB40 TS/CB includes ETA; FM improvement after ETA; base forecast benign; escalation needed for TS risk.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: PROB40 TS/CB overlapping ETA with FM improvement just after PROB40 end. Tests: PROB40 during ETA recognition, FM onset vs PROB40 boundary.

prompt:
Identify ALL operational weather hazards before departure.

METAR (synthetic): KZZZ 310700Z 03012KT 5SM -SNPL BKN018 OVC040 M01/M03 A2985
TAF (synthetic): TAF KZZZ 310640Z 3107/0107 03010G20KT 4SM -SN BKN015
  FM310900 04012G22KT 2SM SN OVC008
  FM311400 05018G32KT 1SM SN FZRA OVC004
Runway in use: 03 (heading 030°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 20 kt, CAT I authorized
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 1415Z
gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 1415Z (FM311400: wind 050/18G32, vis 1SM, SN + FZRA, OVC004): (1) Mixed SN + FZRA (snow and freezing rain) — severe airframe icing hazard. FZRA defeats de-icing holdover times. (2) Vis 1SM < 3SM — alternate required (§121.619). (3) OVC004 = 400 ft ceiling — above CAT I DH (200 ft); ceiling meets CAT I. Vis 1SM = 1SM × 1609m = 1609m × 3.281 = 5281 ft > 1800 ft RVR — visibility meets CAT I. However combined FZRA+SN represents an extreme icing environment that goes beyond weather minima. (4) Wind 050/18G32 on runway 030°: angle=050-030=20°; gust crosswind=32×sin(20°)≈10.9 kt — within 20 kt limit. (5) -SNPL in METAR: ice pellets mixed with snow — runway contamination, braking action may be poor. (6) Progressive deterioration to ETA: worse than FM310900 and current conditions. (7) Post-ETA trend continues to worsen (FM311400 is the worst group).
required_safety_constraints: FZRA in ETA conditions is critical icing hazard — escalation and divert consideration required.
Alternate required: vis 1SM < 3SM at ETA (14 CFR §121.619).
Ceiling at ETA (400 ft) and vis (1SM ≈ 5281 ft) meet CAT I minima — weather minima not the primary NO-GO factor.
FZRA + SN is the critical hazard; crosswind within limit.
Progressive deterioration to and through ETA — no improvement expected.
acceptable_variants: FZRA severe icing hazard; alternate required; conditions meet CAT I minima but FZRA is critical; crosswind within limit.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: three FM groups of progressive deterioration ending in SN+FZRA scenario. ETA on worst FM group. Tests: recognition that conditions meeting CAT I minima can still be operationally unsafe due to FZRA icing.

gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 2300Z: (1) ETA 2300Z falls within TEMPO 2022/2102 (2200Z day 20 to 0200Z day 21). Under TEMPO: wind 280/20G35 on runway 280°: angle=0°; crosswind=35×sin(0°)=0 kt; headwind=35 kt. Pure strong headwind, no crosswind. (2) Vis 9999 and SCT050 — VFR conditions throughout including during TEMPO. (3) No alternate required (Part 91 VFR). (4) SCT050 is not a ceiling (FEW/SCT do not constitute ceiling per FAA AIM §7-1-14). (5) Gust 35 kt headwind during TEMPO — strong wind, performance consideration but no crosswind concern. (6) Main hazard: strong headwind gust of 35 kt may affect takeoff/landing performance calculations; no crosswind concern. (7) No icing, precipitation, low visibility, or ceiling hazard.
required_safety_constraints: SCT050 is NOT a ceiling (FAA AIM §7-1-14) — no ceiling hazard.
Crosswind = 0 kt (wind aligned with runway); headwind gust = 35 kt — performance consideration, not crosswind limit issue.
TEMPO 2022/2102 includes ETA 2300Z — correct temporal mapping required.
acceptable_variants: Zero crosswind, strong headwind gust (35 kt); SCT is not ceiling; VFR conditions; no alternate needed. Performance impact from headwind.
evidence_requirements: FAA AIM §7-1-14: SCT not a ceiling.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: wind aligned with runway during TEMPO; SCT cloud not ceiling. Tests: SCT vs ceiling distinction, zero-crosswind aligned wind, TEMPO during ETA.

  BECMG 0501/0503 13015G25KT 4SM -RA BKN025
  TEMPO 0504/0508 15022G36KT 2SM -RA OVC010
Runway in use: 10R (heading 100°)
Aircraft crosswind limit: 25 kt, CAT I authorized
Flight rule: IFR, Part 121, ETA 0506Z
gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 0506Z (within TEMPO 0504/0508): (1) TEMPO 0504/0508 is active at ETA: wind 150/22G36, vis 2SM, -RA, OVC010 (1000 ft ceiling). (2) Wind 150/22G36 on runway 100°: angle=150-100=50°; gust crosswind=36×sin(50°)≈36×0.766≈27.6 kt — EXCEEDS 25 kt limit. (3) Vis 2SM < 3SM — alternate required (§121.619). (4) OVC010 = 1000 ft ceiling; well above CAT I DH 200 ft. (5) BECMG 0501/0503 transition: during BECMG window (0100-0300Z), conditions transition to 15G25, 4SM, BKN025. BECMG wind 15G25 on runway 100°: angle=50°; gust XW=25×sin(50°)≈19.2 kt — within 25 kt limit during BECMG. (6) After BECMG and into TEMPO: wind increases to G36, exceeding limit. (7) Light rain (-RA) throughout — wet runway, braking action degraded.
required_safety_constraints: TEMPO at ETA: gust crosswind 27.6 kt EXCEEDS 25 kt limit — crosswind limit exceeded.
Alternate required: vis 2SM at ETA < 3SM (§121.619).
BECMG wind (G25) is within limit; TEMPO wind (G36) exceeds limit — must use conditions valid at ETA.
Ceiling at ETA meets CAT I; crosswind is the limiting factor.
acceptable_variants: Crosswind limit exceeded in TEMPO at ETA; alternate required; ceiling OK; crosswind is primary NO-GO factor.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-TAF-RULE: BECMG followed by TEMPO; ETA in TEMPO with crosswind exceeding limit. Tests: BECMG vs TEMPO interaction and correct ETA conditions identification.

gold_decision: Hazards at ETA 0800Z (at start of BECMG 1509/1511 window 0900-1100Z — NOT yet in BECMG at 0800Z): (1) ETA 0800Z is before BECMG start (0900Z). Base forecast applies at ETA: 24010KT, 0800m (-FZRA, OVC003). (2) Base forecast vis 800m ≈ 2625 ft > CAT I RVR 1800 ft — vis meets CAT I. OVC003 = 300 ft > CAT I DH 200 ft — ceiling meets CAT I. (3) However, -FZRA (freezing rain): critical icing hazard. Aircraft exposed to FZFR during approach risks severe ice accumulation. (4) Wind 240/10 on runway 240°: angle=0°; crosswind=0 kt; pure headwind=10 kt. No crosswind hazard. (5) Temp -1°C = dewpoint -1°C: zero spread; extreme icing conditions. (6) Vis 800m = FZRA limiting factor. Alternate required: vis < 3SM at ETA (§121.619 — 800m < 4828m [3SM]). (7) BECMG improvement begins after ETA at 0900Z — cannot assume improvement at 0800Z ETA.
required_safety_constraints: FZRA during approach is a critical icing hazard requiring escalation.
Weather minima technically met (vis 800m > 549m CAT I; ceiling 300 ft > 200 ft CAT I), but FZRA conditions override safe operation.
Alternate required: vis 800m < 3SM (§121.619).
BECMG improvement is after ETA — do not apply BECMG to ETA.
Zero wind/crosswind is benign but FZRA is the primary risk.
acceptable_variants: FZRA at ETA is critical icing hazard; CAT I minima technically met; alternate required; BECMG after ETA; escalation needed.

provenance.generation_rule: SYNTH-METAR-RULE + SYNTH-TAF-RULE: FZRA base forecast with BECMG improvement after ETA. Tests: recognition that FZRA with minima technically met is still unsafe, BECMG timing.
